summary: The Mapbox Static Images thumbnail recipe (style id, path spec, 400x200@2x, padding=30) is duplicated verbatim in two layers: this Twirl helper and RouteBuilder.js #thumbnailUrl (1003-1007).
failure_scenario: Any thumbnail change requires synchronized edits in Scala template and JS; miss one and dashboard cards silently render differently. Backend already computes encoded_polyline per route in RouteService.getRoutesForUser and has the Mapbox key — emit a ready thumbnail_url from one place in RouteWithStats.

summary: Google polyline encode + decimate reimplemented in JS solely so guest saves can build a thumbnail, duplicating app/models/utils/PolylineEncoder.scala including the magic 60-point cap (RouteService.scala:138 vs RouteBuilder.js:1594).
failure_scenario: Precision, zigzag encoding, or decimation cap must be changed in two languages; drift means guest-route thumbnails differ from account-route thumbnails silently. POST /saveRoute response could return encoded_polyline, deleting this file and both hardcoded 60s.

summary: getCityLabelingSpeed near-verbatim copies the per-city body of getCrossCityLabelingSpeed (same getCityLabelingSpeedBySchema call, same hours>0 && km>0 guard, same recover/log), differing only in cache key and unit factor (*60.0 min vs *3600.0 sec per 100m).
failure_scenario: A formula change must be made in two closures whose unit factors differ by 60x — updating one silently skews either RouteBuilder's time estimate or /admin/across-cities. Extract one private labelingSpeedForSchema(schema) in a canonical unit; both public methods convert and cache on top of it.

summary: The route-final-street end-of-task fix is a call-site heuristic (isRoute && no nextTask && walked>=0.9 of street) layered around the fixed 25m END_OF_STREET_THRESHOLD instead of fixing the threshold semantics in Task.isAtEnd.
failure_scenario: Root cause — 25m is most of a short street — still fires on short non-final streets, ending tasks early, and behavior is governed by two magic numbers in two classes plus a route-only special case. Capping the threshold at a fraction of street length inside Task.isAtEnd (e.g. min(25m, 40% of length)) fixes every caller at one chokepoint and removes the isRoute branch.

summary: Share-link construction (`${origin}/r/${slug}` with the /explore?routeId= fallback) is hand-built in three files: here, MyRoutes.js:57-60, and RouteBuilder.js:1592.
failure_scenario: Changing the link shape needs three synchronized edits across two separately-bundled apps; a missed one hands out a dead or inconsistent link. One shared helper in public/js/common/ — or a share_url field returned by the backend next to slug — makes it one edit.

summary: routeDescriptionError is a copy-paste of routeNameError, and both keep ProfanityGuard/length business validation in the controller while sibling features (UserService:414, StoryService:393) run identical checks in the service layer.
failure_scenario: A new rule must be written twice, and RouteService.saveRoute/updateRoute accept unvalidated text if ever called from another entry point. Collapse to one parameterized validator homed in RouteService with the controller only localizing the returned error key, matching UserService/StoryService precedent.

summary: The walking-order rule `routeStreetPosition ?? routeStreetId` is defined as two identical inline lambdas in the same class (getRouteEndpoints:280 and nextTask:331).
failure_scenario: When the legacy routeStreetId fallback is retired, both copies must be edited; updating only one makes the minimap's start/finish flags disagree with the actual next-street walk order. Hoist to one place — a static #walkOrder(task) or Task.getWalkOrder().

summary: The slugify invariant comment anchors a cross-file contract to the wrong evolution: it cites 'evolution 337's backfill' but this PR's backfill (the '--<route_id>' dedupe relying on the no-consecutive-dashes invariant) lives in 344.sql after rebase renumbering.
failure_scenario: A maintainer verifying the coupling opens 337.sql (unrelated shipped evolution) and either wastes time or concludes the invariant is dead and 'simplifies' slugify, re-enabling backfilled-slug collisions. Point the comment (and PR-body references) at 344.sql.
